title: 6 Large Male Fern Garden Ready Plants - Deciduous with Lance-Shaped Fonds. Perfect Shrub for Outdoor Rockery or Cottage Garden, Container Planting or Indoor Houseplant. 1ltr Rootball, 30-40cm Tall
features: 🥬 IMPRESSIVE: The deciduous Male Fern, Dryopteris filix-mas, is robust and hardy. Forming dramatic shuttlecock-like tufts of lance-shaped fronds, reaching up to 1.2 metres in height. Its bipinnatifid fronds create a lush, green rosette that brings a sense of wilderness and natural beauty to any garden setting. Perfect for informal gardens, wildflower meadows, and underplanting in flower borders and beds. It's visually striking, resilient and easy to care for. A true favourite for gardeners.

🥬 PEAT & PLASTIC FREE – These three plants are grown peat free on our nursery which has been peat free for years. Going peat-free helps to conserve peat bog habitats, saving nature and combating climate change. Your plants will arrive without any plastic packaging and without plastic plant pots. We reuse or recycle all of our growing plastic so you don’t have to. You’ll be able to plant the root ball straight into the ground without the pots blowing around your garden.

🥬 MODERN LOOK – These well established, attractive architectural shaped plants have have frond leaves, giving an exotic jungle-like tree appearance. Ferns are one of the oldest groups of plants on Earth, dating back millions of years. They can keep their interesting leaves all year round and enjoy shady places. A perfect perennial looking attractive indoors or outside. Often planted in patio pots and containers.

🥬 CARE – Once planted these ‘easy care’ plants need little maintenance. They like organic matter, like compost or leafmould. Ferns like lots of moisture but not waterlogging. It’s a good idea to trim off the brown leaves in Autumn. Cutting these fronds off will refresh your plant and leave you with beautiful new fronds. The three combined plants will give any garden or home a slick, architectural tropical feel, wowing any visiting guests.

🥬 GARDENING IS GOOD FOR YOU! - Getting outside, digging, and keeping active not only helps your physical wellbeing but also improves your mental health too. Not only that, but the green colour of the leaves and the bright colours of the petals are proven to brighten up people’s mood. So why not pop these in your basket to improve your health and brighten up your family’s winter days.
description: Product Description About Our Ferns Shield Fern is a native of Britain with soft dark green fronds which softly unfurl open. Holly Fern is native to Eastern Asia and is perfect as a house plant or placed outside. Male Fern is a classic fern, originally found in woodlands, growing impressive trends from underground rhizomes. It dyes back in Autumn and comes back each year. Combined, they give any garden or home a slick, architectural tropical feel, wowing any visiting guests. The video showcases the product in use. The video guides you through product setup.
